output.summary: First week post-inception: book returned -0.62% vs SPY +0.95%, alpha -1.56%. Cold-start diversification deployed 6 medium-conviction positions (~41% gross) with heavy cash, which structurally underperformed a beta-up week.
what_worked: Insider-selling exclusion discipline kept us out of META/AAPL/MSFT/JPM/AMD/MU/V on a week where we have no evidence those would have helped — preserves the rule but no proof yet.
Cold-start sizing restraint (no single position >8%) limited downside on a -1.56% alpha week; could have been worse with concentrated NVDA-week exposure.
what_missed: Cash drag was the dominant cost: ~59% cash on a +0.95% SPY week mechanically gives up ~55bps before any stock selection. Cold-start caution overshot.
Avoiding QQQ/SPY/XLK as 'redundant' meant we expressed tech via only NVDA+GOOGL at 13% combined — under-indexed the actual beta driver in a tech-led tape.
Defensive ballast (BRK.B 8%, UNH 6%) at 14% of book is heavy for a risk-on week; the macro-disfavored screen flagged this but we held the names anyway as 'diversifiers'.
Insider-selling flag was applied as a hard veto rather than a sizing input — likely excluded names that worked (MSFT/META in a tech-up week).
lessons_for_pm: Cash >50% on a cold start is a real cost, not a free option: each 1% of SPY upside costs ~55bps of relative performance per 55% cash. After week 1, deploy toward target gross unless a specific risk-off thesis justifies the drag.
Don't treat 'redundancy' with index ETFs as a reason to skip — if single-name conviction is only medium, a small QQQ/SPY sleeve captures the beta you're implicitly betting on anyway.
Reconsider insider-selling as a hard veto. Treat it as a -1 to -2 conviction notch or a size haircut, not exclusion, especially for mega-caps where insider sales are often programmatic (10b5-1).

NVDA-earnings 'binary risk' framing led to under-sizing the highest-conviction setup (technical 74, AI capex intact). When the binary resolves favorably, small size = small reward. Either take the risk at meaningful size or pass.
Healthcare/financial 'ballast' only ballasts in a drawdown. On a flat-to-up week these positions are pure opportunity cost — re-evaluate UNH and BRK.B sizing if the tape stays risk-on.
watch_for: Whether XLE and UNH (uncorrelated diversifiers) actually decorrelate or just drag — measure week-2 contribution explicitly.
Insider-selling veto false-negatives: track what MSFT/META/AAPL did this week to calibrate whether the rule is too strict.
Cash deployment pace — set a target gross exposure for week 2 before scanning names, not as a residual.
